patches.find: monitor script hardcodes the target number on the same commit as the data-fetch line, so the temporal ordering the definition requires cannot be checked from the repository history.
patches.replace: monitor script hardcodes both the predicted and the observed values — there is no independent data-fetch step, so the temporal ordering the definition requires cannot be checked at all.
patches.rationale: WIN-009 has monitoring=hardcoded with no data-fetch line. The 'data-fetch line' phrasing implies a separate fetch exists alongside the hardcoded value. For hardcoded-no-fetch WINs, both values are compiled in with no retrieval step.

patches.find: Chave & Jones, 2012). The robust first harmonic
patches.replace: Chave & Jones, 2012). MT (magnetotelluric) signals at ELF frequencies are dominated by the same Earth–ionosphere cavity excitations measured atmospherically as Schumann modes; the underground spectrum is the surface spectrum coupled into the ground, not an independent resonance. The robust first harmonic
patches.rationale: Adds bridge sentence preempting the defender argument that telluric MT measurements are distinct from atmospheric Schumann — they are the same physical modes observed via different sensors. Closes the strawman lane identified in WIN-009.c3.json hole #2.

patches.find: (IGRF, NASA station archives, ocean-tide gauges, telluric records, etc.)
patches.replace: published MT and ELF spectral literature
patches.rationale: IGRF, NASA station archives, and ocean-tide gauges are irrelevant to a telluric/ELF WIN. Replace generic boilerplate source list with WIN-009-specific description matching WIN-008's phrasing.

patches.find: The globe's distant moon produces two nearly symmetric bulges (near-side and far-side) producing the observed once-per-lunar-day diurnal tidal pattern across all ocean basins simultaneously.
patches.replace: O1 arises from the moon's declination ≈ ±28.6° relative to the equatorial plane producing once-per-day inequality between successive semidiurnal high tides — a smooth latitude-dependent modulation requiring a distant moon (d/R >> 1).
patches.rationale: Two-bulge mechanism describes M2 semidiurnal (two high tides per day), not O1 diurnal. Prior run fixed TLDRs and detail_verdict_text but missed this sentence in detail_evidence. Replaces with correct diurnal mechanism description.
